reasoning_summary: The AI engine could generate $80k-$140k extra revenue monthly (40% margin = $32k-$56k gross profit). After subtracting $30k monthly operating costs, net benefit ranges from $2k to $26k. At the low end, payback of the $180k implementation cost would take ~90 months, far beyond the 12-month requirement; at the high end it would be ~7 months, meeting the target. Because the outcome is uncertain and the worst-case fails the leadership criterion, approval should be conditional on securing a higher-confidence uplift (e.g., minimum 5% revenue lift) or reducing costs.
key_assumptions: Revenue uplift of 4%-7% applies consistently each month after go-live.
Gross margin remains constant at 40% for incremental sales.
Monthly AI infrastructure ($22k) and maintenance ($8k) costs are fixed.
No additional hidden costs or revenue cannibalization.
Implementation cost is incurred upfront and not amortized.
